title: Television control experiment (30 subjects, 4 classes, 31 EEG ch)
description: A P300-based brain-computer interface dataset for television control comprising EEG recordings from 30 healthy subjects performing a visual oddball task. The dataset includes 31-channel EEG data acquired at 500 Hz using active electrodes in a standard 10-20 montage, with target and non-target stimulus classifications for home appliance control applications. Data were collected in an online feedback paradigm with visual flash stimuli presented at 750 ms stimulus onset asynchrony.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using an actiCHamp system (Brain Products) with 31 active electrodes in a standard 10-20 montage, referenced to linked mastoids, sampled at 500 Hz with 60 Hz line frequency notch. Subjects performed a P300 oddball task with visual flash stimuli on an LCD display. Each subject completed one session with 50 training and 30 testing blocks, with trial duration of 1.0 s and stimulus onset asynchrony of 750 ms.
